conditions.message: The entry reduces reward-hacking risk by preventing a superficial feature-complete declaration from excluding required orchestrator-context work; it names exactly what must land before the preflip gate and explicitly prevents reopening unrelated feature work.
evidence: D-OSL-19 states that four orchestrator-experience template beads join launch scope and must land before the preflip gate.
The named beads cover C5 polling standard, contact-mesh pre-approval, Agent Mail registration mandate at dispatch, and orchestrator no-self-execution role split.
The entry ties the decision to first-time Burpvalve users receiving pre-built orchestrator context with documented polling, contact-mesh, registration, and role-split rules.
The final sentence states: No other feature work is reopened by this amendment.
